title: Silky Earthy Thai Oysters Curry
description: A refined, romantic curry featuring briny oysters in a velvety coconut-turmeric base with earthy undertones of fermented bean paste. Developed with kosher-friendly swaps to ensure a luxurious, shellfish-focused profile without dairy or non-kosher ferments.

ingredients.name: Fresh Shucked Oysters
ingredients.notes: Reserve 2 tablespoons of the oyster liquor

ingredients.name: Full-fat Coconut Milk
ingredients.notes: Do not shake; use the thick cream from the top

ingredients.name: Yellow Curry Paste
ingredients.notes: Ensure no shrimp paste is included for kosher-style

ingredients.name: Yellow Miso Paste
ingredients.notes: Provides the 'earthy' fermented element in place of shrimp paste

ingredients.name: Shallots
ingredients.notes: Thinly sliced and fried until golden

instructions: Spoon 3 tablespoons of the thick coconut cream into a wok or heavy saucepan over medium heat, stirring until it splits and releases its oil.
Add the yellow curry paste and grated turmeric to the oil, frying for 2 minutes until fragrant and deepened in color.
Whisk in the yellow miso paste and palm sugar, ensuring the ferment is well incorporated into the aromatic base.
Pour in the remaining coconut milk and the reserved oyster liquor, then add the galangal slices.
Simmer the sauce gently for 8-10 minutes on low heat until it reduces slightly and achieves a silky, coating consistency.
Remove the galangal slices and discard; taste the sauce for a balance of earthy, salty, and sweet.
Carefully slide the fresh oysters into the simmering sauce, poaching them for exactly 2 minutes until the edges just begin to curl.
Turn off the heat and fold in the fresh Thai basil leaves and lime juice.
Serve immediately in shallow bowls, garnished generously with the golden fried shallots.
